why_gold: It demonstrates a core one-person company principle: sharp scope and low operational complexity can beat broad feature competition.
reusable_lessons: A crowded market is not automatically bad if the job is narrower than incumbents serve.
Feature limitation can be a strategic moat when it improves speed, clarity, and price.
A product-led free tier can create distribution if the output naturally travels.
warning_flags: Do not copy the product category without copying the discipline of scope control.
Large free-user bases can create support load before revenue quality is proven.

why_gold: It gives a realistic solo-founder path from service revenue to a narrow subscription product, avoiding the false fantasy of quitting first and validating later.
reusable_lessons: Service work can finance product development without outside capital.
A niche subscription app needs a user group with repeated workflow pain.
Full-time transition should follow revenue evidence, not emotional conviction.
warning_flags: This route is slow and demands product maintenance stamina.
Do not treat public writing as marketing unless it reaches the same people who buy.

why_gold: It is a concrete China-market example of using Xiaohongshu as a validation and early-user surface for a consumer app.
reusable_lessons: For visually explainable consumer products, Xiaohongshu can test resonance before paid acquisition.
Comments and saves can reveal language, objections, and scenario fit.
Early attention should be converted into retention evidence before building a bigger feature set.
warning_flags: Platform traction is not payment validation.
Consumer app retention can collapse after novelty fades.
